case_metadata.case_name: Jesus Virlar, M.D. and Gmg Health Systems Associates, P.A., A/K/A and D/B/A Gonzaba Medical Group v. Jo Ann Puente

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: In 2011, respondent Jo Ann Puente underwent 'Roux-en-Y' gastric-bypass surgery performed by Dr. Nilesh Patel.
evidence_index.summary: Jo Ann Puente underwent gastric-bypass surgery in 2011.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Puente was admitted to intensive care at Metropolitan Methodist Hospital and ordered to take nothing by mouth.
evidence_index.summary: Puente was admitted to intensive care and ordered NPO.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Dr. Jesus Virlar, who was employed by petitioner GMG Health Systems Associates, P.A. ('Gonzaba'), assumed care for Puente.
evidence_index.summary: Dr. Virlar assumed care for Puente.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Evidence at trial showed that, although nurses noted Puente’s difficulty walking, dizziness, continued vomiting, and 'fixed gaze,' Dr. Virlar did not read their notes and was unaware of the symptoms.
evidence_index.summary: Dr. Virlar was unaware of Puente's symptoms noted by nurses.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Dr. Virlar failed to order thiamine supplements, which Puente’s expert witness testified led her to develop Wernicke’s disease, a brain dysfunction associated with thiamine deficiency.
evidence_index.summary: Expert testified failure to order thiamine led to Wernicke’s disease.
evidence_index.speaker: Puente's expert witness

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Without the supplements, the disease progressed to a more debilitating brain disorder, Korsakoff’s syndrome.
evidence_index.summary: Wernicke’s disease progressed to Korsakoff’s syndrome.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Puente died while this appeal was pending.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The jury found Drs. Virlar and Patel negligent, and it found Dr. Virlar 60% responsible and Dr. Patel 40% responsible for Puente’s injuries.
evidence_index.summary: Jury found Dr. Virlar 60% and Dr. Patel 40% responsible.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The jury awarded Puente $133,202 for past earnings lost, $888,420 for future earning capacity lost, and $13,262,874.86 for future medical expenses.
evidence_index.summary: Jury awarded Puente over $14 million in damages.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: We hold that Chapter 33 required a credit for the daughter’s settlement because her claims were for her mother’s injury and that this result does not violate the Open Courts provision of the Texas Constitution.
evidence_index.summary: Chapter 33 requires credit for daughter's settlement; no Open Courts violation.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: On this record, the TMLA required the trial court to order that at least some of the future damages be paid periodically.
evidence_index.summary: TMLA requires periodic payment of future damages.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Because Puente has not lost a common-law remedy, we conclude that the Open Courts provision has not been violated and that C.P.’s settlement should be credited against the judgment.
evidence_index.summary: Open Courts provision not violated; C.P.'s settlement credited.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: We reverse the judgment in part and remand the case to the trial court for further proceedings to form a proper judgment on these issues of damages.
evidence_index.summary: Judgment reversed in part; remanded for proper judgment on damages.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The trial court’s failure to award any periodic payments was error, and we reverse the portion of the judgment awarding all of the damages for future medical expenses in a lump sum.
evidence_index.summary: Error in not awarding periodic payments; judgment reversed in part.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

verdict_detected.key_phrase: We reverse the judgment in part and remand the case to the trial court for further proceedings to form a proper judgment on these issues of damages.

gaps_identified: Exact timing of symptom onset unclear

initial_state.chief_complaint: Difficulty walking, dizziness, continued vomiting, and 'fixed gaze'
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient had undergone Roux-en-Y gastric-bypass surgery and was admitted to intensive care.

focused_exam.neurological: Difficulty walking, dizziness, continued vomiting, and 'fixed gaze'

requestables.name: Thiamine Level
requestables.description: Measurement of thiamine levels to assess deficiency

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in ICU with neurological symptoms post-surgery
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management?

options.clinical_reasoning: Thiamine deficiency suspected; supplementation is critical
Failed to address potential thiamine deficiency

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to prescribe thiamine led to progression of disease
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Thiamine supplementation should have been initiated

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed Wernicke's disease progressing to Korsakoff's syndrome

key_findings: Causation established - progression to Korsakoff's syndrome

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Thiamine supplementation would have prevented progression

diagnoses.diagnosis: Wernicke's disease progressing to Korsakoff's syndrome

court_findings.finding: Dr. Virlar breached duty of care
